articles.title: EU Gives Ukraine a Giant $106 Billion Lifeline — Here's Why It Matters
articles.summary: Ukraine just got a huge $106 billion loan from the European Union to help keep its government running and fight the war. The money was held up for months because Hungary's leader, Viktor Orbán, who is friendly with Russia, blocked it. But Orbán lost an election, and the deal was finally approved after Ukraine fixed an oil pipeline that supplies Hungary and Slovakia. Without this money, Ukraine might have run out of cash this spring. About a third of the funds will help pay for things like salaries and hospitals, while the rest goes to buying weapons and making more in Ukraine. The loan is so big it could cover two-thirds of what Ukraine needs for the next two years.…

articles.title: Court Says Trump Can't Ban Asylum Seekers at the Border — Here's Why
articles.summary: A federal appeals court just ruled that President Trump's order to stop people from asking for asylum at the southern border is illegal. The court said that U.S. immigration laws give people the right to apply for asylum, and the president can't just override that. Trump had declared the situation at the border an "invasion" and tried to suspend all asylum requests. But the judges said that's not how the law works. The White House says it will keep fighting the ruling. But advocates for immigrants say this is a huge victory for people fleeing war and danger who deserve a chance to tell their story. The ruling doesn't take effect right away — the government can still appeal to…

articles.title: US and Iran Might Talk — But Will They? Envoys Head to Pakistan
articles.summary: The US is sending two top envoys, Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, to Pakistan to meet with Iran's foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi. The goal? To try to start talks about ending the US-Israeli war on Iran and deal with tensions over the Strait of Hormuz, a super important waterway for oil shipments. But here's the catch: Iran hasn't agreed to meet yet. Iran's foreign minister said he's going to Pakistan, but didn't mention any plans to talk to the Americans. The US has been putting pressure on Iran, including freezing $344 million in cryptocurrency assets. There's already a fragile ceasefire in place, but both sides are still in a standoff. Will they talk? Nobody knows yet.
